blocks.content: The call lasted an hour. Mostly Eli telling the story of the street corner — the Mandarin he'd practiced in secret for three weeks, the tones that were wrong, and the please that wasn't planned — and Meilin laughing through crying, and Kit listening.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I practiced for three weeks. My tones were completely wrong.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: What did she say about that.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: She said I got the important parts right.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: It came out. I didn't plan it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: It's the best part of the story.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: She thinks so too. She told her mom tonight and led with the Mandarin. I am now known as the man who tried.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Worse things to be known as.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: We're going to be in each other's weddings.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Was there ever a version where we weren't.
Kit smiled at the ceiling of his dorm room in LA while his best friend did the same thing in Millhaven.
